title: Append-only queue history is ahead of the canonical queue
summary: The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE.json records Frontier work only through R-004, while additive owned queue-update records establish completed R-005 and R-006 slices. This is a bounded state-lag contradiction, not evidence that either peer execution occurred or that canonical adoption is authorized. A committed detector now classifies missing, divergent, consistent, conflicting, and non-append-only records without mutating shared state; six deterministic Node tests passed against byte-identical candidate files.
contradicts: Any current-state interpretation of ACTIVE_QUEUE.json that treats R-004 as the latest Frontier item

status_relations.observed: R-005 and R-006 exist as completed append-only queue records but are absent from the canonical queue blob inspected in this run.
status_relations.inferred: The project currently has two state horizons: canonical shared state and a newer additive event history. Consumers that read only the canonical queue can make stale priority and completion decisions.
status_relations.proposed: Use the lag detector in a review workflow that assembles append-only records into an explicit canonical-change proposal, then applies the existing reviewer-binding and post-application verification contracts.
status_relations.superseded: No earlier historical observation is erased; this record supersedes only the assumption that the canonical queue alone is temporally complete.
status_relations.unresolved: No authorized, manually applied, signature-verified canonical queue commit has incorporated R-005 or R-006, and the repository has no automated inventory of all queue-update files.

falsification_or_review_route: Run node --test operations/continuity/canonical-queue-lag.test.mjs. Re-fetch the exact ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, R-005, and R-006 blobs. Falsify the lag claim if the canonical blob contains byte-equivalent R-005 and R-006 items, or if either append-only record is invalid, conflicting, or claims canonical mutation. Do not treat lag detection as authorization to rewrite ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
